item.title: Tensioni Iran-Trump pesano sui mercati
item.description: Le tensioni tra Iran e Trump stanno influenzando negativamente i mercati, con un aumento della volatilità e un calo degli asset rischiosi.

item.title: La guerra in Iran potrebbe fermare il boom dell'AI
item.description: La guerra in Iran potrebbe avere un impatto negativo sul boom dell'intelligenza artificiale, a causa della dipendenza dalle importazioni di energia e prodotti chimici dal Medio Oriente.

item.title: La sicurezza economica in discussione
item.description: Il Peterson Institute for International Economics presenterà una discussione di alto livello sulla sicurezza economica e sui compromessi nel mondo geopoliticamente fratturato.

item.title: China touts itself as ‘harbour of stability’ to global CEOs
item.description: Premier Li Qiang paints contrast to US at business gathering in Beijing as Donald Trump gets bogged down in Iran

item.title: Iran war fallout set to filter into economic data
item.description: Telling PMI, consumer confidence and inflation updates, plus Denmark goes to the polls, Anglicans anoint first female leader

item.title: War with Iran has exposed American fragility at sea
item.description: The move from ‘efficiency’ to ‘resilience’ in maritime supply chains has a long way to go

item.title: Amid War, Yemen’s New Government Faces Challenges at Home and Abroad
item.description: Yemen has a new government, formed after elements of the old one clashed on the battlefield. In this Q&A, Crisis Group expert Ahmed Nagi looks at its makeup and its prospects against the backdrop of Middle East war that may draw in the Houthi rebels.

item.title: Where is the Israel-Hizbollah War Going?
item.description: Israel and Hizbollah are once again fighting head to head, with disastrous repercussions for Lebanon. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Heiko Wimmen, David Wood and Max Rodenbeck consider what might come next.

item.title: An Unserious Justification for an Unnecessary War: Assessing the U.S. “Article 51” Letter to U.N. on Iran War
item.description: An Unserious Justification for an Unnecessary War: Assessing the U.S. “Article 51” Letter to U.N. on Iran War

item.title: China's e-trucks poised to dominate nascent EU market
item.description: China's e-trucks poised to dominate nascent EU market

item.title: War in the Middle East exposes high-flying Seoul stock market's vulnerability
item.description: War in the Middle East exposes high-flying Seoul stock market's vulnerability

item.title: Middle East oil shock hits home for smaller Japanese companies
item.description: Middle East oil shock hits home for smaller Japanese companies

item.title: Trump’s Iran Uranium Dilemma Raises Stakes for Oil Markets
item.description: Trump faces a simple but difficult choice: Iran’s enriched uranium still needs to be secured, and airstrikes haven’t accomplished that. The only clear way to control it would likely require U.S. troops on the ground—an option that risks widening the conflict and raising the stakes significantly. That uncertainty has been pouring over into global energy markets. Oil prices have pulled back from recent highs but remain elevated as the Middle East conflict shows no signs of abating. Europe remains largely non-committal towards using…

item.title: Nuclear in the Spotlight amid Oil, Gas Crunch
item.description: Nuclear energy has been making a comeback recently as one of the low-carbon baseload generation options. This comeback is set to accelerate amid the Middle Eastern crisis and the oil and gas squeeze it is causing. The only problem is that nuclear capacity takes a while to build. “It was a strategic mistake for Europe to turn its back on a reliable, affordable source of low-emission power,” European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen admitted earlier this month at a nuclear energy summit in France. Von der Leyen herself had voted…

item.title: Oil Markets Brace for Volatility as Trump’s Iran Deadline Fuels Escalation Fears
item.description: Oil prices opened the week elevated but stable in early Asian trading on Monday, holding near multi-year highs after another weekend of escalating tensions between the United States and Iran. At the time of writing, WTI crude was trading at $98.61, up 0.39%, while Brent crude had inched down 0.24% to trade at $111.90. These muted price moves mask an increasingly uncertain environment, with markets bracing for a very volatile session. Late on Saturday, President Trump issued a stark ultimatum, warning that the United States would strike Iran’s…

item.title: Hong Kong’s Boyaa Interactive eyes $70M crypto treasury expansion
item.description: Boyaa Interactive International is the 23rd-largest Bitcoin treasury and the third-largest in Asia, behind Japan’s Metaplanet and China’s Next Technology Holding.

item.title: Crypto, stocks fall as oil chops after Iran vows response to Trump threat
item.description: Bitcoin and Asian markets dropped on Monday as Iran escalated threats to strike critical infrastructure in the Gulf.

item.title: BTC and gold divergence reflects split between retail and central banks: Analyst
item.description: 21Shares' macro chief looks at why Bitcoin has held relatively steady since the start of Middle East hostilities, while gold has slipped below $4,500 and key support levels.

item.title: Michael Saylor signals BTC buy as Strategy's stack slips 10% into the red
item.description: Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin-holding company Strategy has already purchased more than $2.9 billion worth of the cryptocurrency this month.
